text: It started as a small side project on Saturday mornings.
My colleague Tom had always been keen on baking, and he began selling cakes at the local market.
He barely made enough to cover the cost of ingredients.
But Tom was patient and dedicated.
He listened to feedback, modified his recipes, and steadily improved the quality.
Within a few months, a surge in orders forced him to rethink his approach.
He launched a simple website and started taking orders online.
The transition from hobby to small business was not easy.
Tom had to negotiate with suppliers and tackle problems he had never anticipated.
Yet the setbacks only made him more determined.
Today, his bakery delivers across the city.
When I asked him what made the difference, he said it was the commitment to keep going, even when the outcome was uncertain.
